step_by_step_thinking:
Based on the thorough review of the conditions presented, my agreement with the final summary holds strong. Post-infectious glomerulonephritis typically manifests differently from those prone to nephrotic syndrome, especially concerning proteinuria levels. While some cases might present mildly, the primary differential feature lies precisely in the absence of nephrotic-range proteinuria—a defining aspect of true nephrotic syndrome. Therefore, maintaining consistency with earlier conclusions, I concur that "B" correctly identifies post-infectious glomerulonephritis as distinct regarding nephrotic syndrome manifestation patterns.

Answer Choice: B.
